name: omarchy_windows_vm
content:
Windows VM 
Omarchy offers an easy way to run Windows through a Docker VM. You can insta it using
Insta > Windows from the Omarchy menu (Super + Alt + Space). It takes a whie, but
you can foow the progress in the browser.
When the instaation is compete, you can aunch Windows using the app auncher, and
that' give you a RDP connection to your instaation. There's no GPU passthrough with
this setup, so it's not suitabe for gaming or video editing, but it's a great way to run apps
ike Microsoft Office or whatever ese if you absoutey must have that.
The directory ~/Windows  in your home directory is automaticay shared with this Windows
VM. So you put fies there if you want to make them accessibe to Windows. The VM does
not have access to any other fies on your system, so you're safe from maware or viruses
on the Linux side.
You can change the resource aocation by editing ~/.config/windows/docker-
compose.yml. You can aso edit this fie to mount USB devices. See a the options on the
Dockur Windows project.
The version of Windows instaed is 11 Pro. It's unactivated. You wi need to activate with
your own icense key to use a gated features.
Windows VM · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/100/windows-vm
